alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs with cross-functional stakeholder engagement
alignments.job_requirement: Cross-functional work with Product, Commercial, Operations, Data, Legal on high-risk cases and strategic commercial opportunities

alignments.candidate_evidence: Strategy and Operations leader experienced in executive-level reporting and governance communications within complex, regulated environments
alignments.job_requirement: Prepare high-quality MI and commentary for local leadership, risk/audit committees and Boards, including key risk indicators and metrics

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation program experience and process design capability, but limited evidence of regulatory compliance program design or financial crime domain knowledge
alignments.job_requirement: Drive ongoing uplift of AML/CTF Program in line with Australian regulation and group-wide standards; partner across divisions on process remediation and control design

gaps.gap: No demonstrated financial crime compliance or AML/CTF program experience; candidate background is commercial operations and product transformation, not regulatory compliance
gaps.mitigation: Structured onboarding and mentorship from experienced compliance practitioners; formal AML/CTF training and AUSTRAC familiarization; early shadowing of SMR/IFTI investigations and regulatory interactions

gaps.gap: No stated auditing, assurance, or direct regulator engagement experience; role explicitly requires AUSTRAC interaction and demonstrated audit/inspection background
gaps.mitigation: Partner with internal audit and risk functions; attend AUSTRAC reviews and interactions with senior compliance leadership support; formal audit and compliance certification pathways

gaps.gap: No deep familiarity with Australian AML/CTF laws, rules, digital payments compliance, or cross-border flow regulations
gaps.mitigation: Dedicated compliance law and fintech regulatory training; detailed policy and procedure documentation review; structured knowledge transfer from global FCC peers

gaps.gap: Limited fintech/payments domain exposure; candidate experience centers on Google's commercial and device ecosystems, not financial services or payment systems
gaps.mitigation: Immersion in Airwallex's payments and embedded finance products; liaison with product and commercial teams on compliance use-cases; external fintech/payments compliance community engagement

output.level_rationale: Role requires 5+ years financial crime compliance in financial institutions or fintech, hands-on investigation and regulatory reporting experience, senior oversight, policy design, and board-level reporting. These responsibilities and seniority anchors (senior manager title, second-line advisor role, MLRO deputy function) align with L5 senior IC ownership. No manager-of-managers scope stated, and company scale (2,200+ employees, $8B valuation) provides no additional stretch; L5 is appropriate.
output.summary: Senior Compliance Manager & Deputy MLRO is a specialist financial crime compliance role in the Regulatory & Compliance department at a mid-stage fintech (Airwallex, $8B, 2,200+ employees). The role requires 5+ years of hands-on AML/CTF program execution, SMR/IFTI filing and investigation, AUSTRAC interaction, and deep Australian regulatory knowledge—none of which align with the candidate's Strategy and Operations background at Google. While the candidate brings strong cross-functional stakeholder management and governance communication skills, the domain expertise gap is substantial and the function sits outside target strategy/operations families. Role family fit is poor; gaps are high-severity and not readily mitigated in early tenure.
uncertainties: Candidate's exact scope of regulatory engagement at Google Devices and Services is not detailed; may have some compliance-adjacent exposure not explicitly stated in profile
Whether candidate has pursued or passed any compliance certifications (CAMS, ACAMS, ICA, etc.) is not disclosed
Depth of candidate's stakeholder engagement with financial regulators or auditors during commercial operations work is unclear
